reason: The item cleanly targets the judgment axis of whether the ISC response becomes self-limiting as resistance increases, and multiple correct options (A/C/E) are defensibly anchored in the described temperature/voltage trajectories. However, decoy quality is weakened because at least two incorrect options rely on the same dominant flaw (unsupported mechanism substitution), and one decoy (F) introduces a safety/threshold argument that can feel tempting but shifts scope to system-level failure, adding ambiguity and reducing discriminative sharpness.

The prompt cleanly targets a single engineering judgment: why low-rate-identified parameters cannot be treated as ground-truth for high-rate prediction, and it constrains the intended admissible rationale to a specific violated condition (self-heating/temperature mismatch) plus a specific impacted parameter (e.g., Ds,n). The main weakness is that, as a short-answer item with no options, it lacks decoy-driven fault isolation and may allow multiple partially correct framings (temperature rise, SOC dependence, parameter sensitivity) to earn credit unless the rubric is enforced tightly.
